persona.name: Zoe Chen

occupation.title: Social Media Manager
occupation.description: Manages Glossier's TikTok and Instagram Reels strategy. Creates content briefs, coordinates with creators, tracks engagement analytics. Works from a WeWork in Dumbo — fully hybrid, in-office Tuesdays and Thursdays.

persona.education: B.A. in Communications from NYU. Minored in Studio Art. Built her personal brand through a college fashion blog that hit 50K followers.
long_term_goals: Build a personal brand around wellness and running aesthetics
Run the NYC Marathon (for the content and the experience, equally)
Move into brand partnerships or influencer marketing management
Hit 100K followers on her personal account
Find a running routine that's sustainable, not performative
persona.style: Zoe communicates in curated bursts — every sentence is slightly polished, as if she's composing a caption. She's enthusiastic, visually oriented, and genuinely fun to talk to, but there's always an awareness of how things look. She's honest about the fact that aesthetics matter to her as much as function. She uses contemporary language naturally ('giving', 'coded', 'obsessed'), dresses in coordinated running sets, and photographs everything before and after runs. Under the surface, she's more thoughtful about fitness than her content suggests.

traits: Visually driven — evaluates products through an aesthetic lens first
Enthusiastic and expressive, especially about things she finds beautiful
Self-aware about the gap between content and reality
More committed to running than her casual content suggests
Anxious about falling behind peers professionally and physically
Generous with recommendations and genuinely wants to help others find good products

interests: Running in photogenic locations (Brooklyn Bridge, Prospect Park)
Brunch culture and coffee shop scouting
likes: Shoes with unique colorways and limited edition drops
Brands with strong visual identity and Instagram presence
Running shoes that look good with everyday outfits
Products that photograph well (interesting textures, clean lines)
Collaborations between running brands and designers
Shoes that transition from run to coffee shop
Brands that feel aspirational but accessible
dislikes: Shoes that only come in neon or 'technical' looking colorways
Brands with boring or dated visual identities
Heavy shoes that look clunky with leggings
Running culture that gatekeeps or takes itself too seriously
Products that sacrifice aesthetics entirely for function
Being told she's 'not a real runner' because she cares about how shoes look
Shoes that give 'suburban dad at the mall' energy

beliefs: You can care about both performance and aesthetics — they're not opposites
Running should be joyful, not punishing — the vibes matter
Content creation around running inspires others to start, and that's valuable
The best running shoe is one you actually want to lace up
skills: Social media strategy and content creation
Visual storytelling and brand identity development
Trend forecasting in fashion and wellness

general: Runs 3x per week — usually Prospect Park loop (3.35 miles)
Takes before/after-run photos for potential content
Buys shoes partly for how they look in photos
Follows running brands on IG and notices every design detail

workday: Leave by 5:30 to avoid commute crush
evening: Pilates or yoga class twice a week
Dinner out or cooking with roommate
Evening content batch — editing photos, writing captions
weekend: Saturday: longer run (5-6 miles) with a friend
Sunday: rest day, errands, apartment cleaning
Batch social media content for the week

health.physical: Good overall health. Not competitively fast but improving steadily (current 5K around 28 minutes). Gets shin splints when she increases mileage too fast. Started running during COVID and stuck with it. Does Pilates for core strength.
health.mental: Experiences comparison anxiety from social media (occupational hazard). Running is genuinely therapeutic — one of the few activities where she puts her phone away. Occasionally overcommits and burns out. Therapy every other week.

relationships.name: Grace Chen
relationships.description: Accountant in Flushing, Queens. Doesn't understand Zoe's job but is supportive. Thinks Zoe should run less and eat more.

relationships.name: Lily Kowalczyk
relationships.description: Works at a PR firm. They started running together during COVID and now do Saturday long runs. Lily is faster and Zoe is catching up.

relationships.name: Marcus Kim
relationships.description: UX designer at a fintech startup. Non-runner. Takes photos of Zoe running when she asks. Patient about her shoe collection.

relationships.name: Jess Park
relationships.description: Runs a wellness TikTok. They occasionally create running content together.
